title: Q2 renewal forecast lands within two percentage points of commit
anchor: Actual ARR at $1.77M versus forecast $1.8M, a 1.7% variance on the $1.8M renewing book.
connect: Trailing four quarters averaged 8.2% variance, with Q2 sitting well inside that range.
body: Actual ARR at $1.77M versus forecast $1.8M, a 1.7% variance on the $1.8M renewing book. Trailing four quarters averaged 8.2% variance, with Q2 sitting well inside that range.
trace: Q2 forecast accuracy significantly improved versus historical baseline, indicating better pipeline visibility and commit discipline.

title: Mid-market NRR jumps seven percentage points above trailing baseline
anchor: Q2 mid-market NRR sits at 112% versus the three-quarter average of 104.9%.
connect: Multi-product mid-market customers concentrate 18 of 24 accounts running Atlas Insights, and 18 of those expanded this quarter.
body: Q2 mid-market NRR sits at 112% versus the three-quarter average of 104.9%. Multi-product mid-market customers concentrate 18 of 24 accounts running Atlas Insights, and 18 of those expanded this quarter.
trace: Mid-market segment showing strong expansion momentum, with Atlas Insights adoption correlating with expansion activity.

title: Multi-product NRR advantage widens to 22 percentage points
anchor: Multi-product customers at 124% NRR across 46 accounts versus single-product customers at 102% across 74 accounts.
connect: Multi-product share sits at 38.3% of the customer base currently.
body: Multi-product customers at 124% NRR across 46 accounts versus single-product customers at 102% across 74 accounts. Multi-product share sits at 38.3% of the customer base currently.
trace: Clear NRR advantage for multi-product customers, with the gap widening from Q1's 14-point spread.

title: At-risk ARR pool contracts by $90K as four accounts clear review
anchor: April at-risk pool sits at $220K across 3 accounts versus March at $310K across 7 accounts.
connect: Top-20 ARR accounts in at-risk status dropped from 5 to 2 over the same period.
body: April at-risk pool sits at $220K across 3 accounts versus March at $310K across 7 accounts. Top-20 ARR accounts in at-risk status dropped from 5 to 2 over the same period.
trace: Significant improvement in at-risk portfolio, with high-value accounts clearing risk status.

title: Time to first value in mid-market compresses by 15 days quarter over quarter
anchor: Q2 mid-market TTFV at 23 days across 24 accounts versus Q1 at 38 days across 22 accounts.
connect: Q1 cohort renewal patterns show under-30-day TTFV correlates with 91% renewal versus 74% for over-60-day TTFV.
body: Q2 mid-market TTFV at 23 days across 24 accounts versus Q1 at 38 days across 22 accounts. Q1 cohort renewal patterns show under-30-day TTFV correlates with 91% renewal versus 74% for over-60-day TTFV.
trace: Significant onboarding improvement in mid-market, with TTFV now consistently under the 30-day renewal-predictive threshold.

title: License utilization above 80% jumps from 14 to 22 accounts in mid-market
anchor: Q2 mid-market customers above 80% license utilization reach 22 accounts versus Q1 at 14 accounts.
connect: Mid-market NRR sits at 112% for Q2, up from the trailing three-quarter average of 104.9%.
body: Q2 mid-market customers above 80% license utilization reach 22 accounts versus Q1 at 14 accounts. Mid-market NRR sits at 112% for Q2, up from the trailing three-quarter average of 104.9%.
trace: Sharp increase in license utilization coinciding with mid-market NRR improvement, suggesting usage drives expansion.

title: Health score distribution shifts seven percentage points toward green
anchor: Q2 mid-market green health at 77% versus Q1 at 70%, representing 34 of 44 accounts.
connect: Q1 green-health renewal rate was 97% versus yellow-health at 78%.
body: Q2 mid-market green health at 77% versus Q1 at 70%, representing 34 of 44 accounts. Q1 green-health renewal rate was 97% versus yellow-health at 78%.
trace: Health score improvement in mid-market aligning with stronger renewal predictability patterns.

title: Coverage tier retention spread sits at 14 percentage points
anchor: Q2 high-touch GRR at 96% across 22 accounts versus tech-touch at 82% across 98 accounts.
connect: High-touch represents 18.3% of the customer base but carries the top ARR accounts.
body: Q2 high-touch GRR at 96% across 22 accounts versus tech-touch at 82% across 98 accounts. High-touch represents 18.3% of the customer base but carries the top ARR accounts.
trace: Persistent retention advantage for high-touch coverage, with the gap holding steady from Q1's 8-point spread.

title: Customer health review expansion acceptance runs at 83%
anchor: March health review opportunities totaled 6, with sales accepting 5 for pipeline development.
connect: Outbound expansion opportunities over the same period ran at 41% acceptance across 17 sourced.
body: March health review opportunities totaled 6, with sales accepting 5 for pipeline development. Outbound expansion opportunities over the same period ran at 41% acceptance across 17 sourced.
trace: Health review sourcing significantly outperforms outbound expansion in sales qualification and acceptance.

title: New customer 90-day retention climbs to 88% with TTFV compression
anchor: Q1 2026 new-customer cohort 90-day retention at 88% across 60 accounts with TTFV at 26 days.
connect: Q4 2025 cohort ran at 83% retention across 55 accounts with TTFV at 38 days.
body: Q1 2026 new-customer cohort 90-day retention at 88% across 60 accounts with TTFV at 26 days. Q4 2025 cohort ran at 83% retention across 55 accounts with TTFV at 38 days.
trace: Clear correlation between faster time to value and improved early-stage retention across new customer cohorts.

title: Enterprise renewal depth strengthens with executive sponsor engagement
anchor: April enterprise renewals total 10 signed, with 7 showing executive sponsor depth increase during Q1.
connect: Enterprise GRR holds at 94% for Q2 versus mid-market at 91%.
body: April enterprise renewals total 10 signed, with 7 showing executive sponsor depth increase during Q1. Enterprise GRR holds at 94% for Q2 versus mid-market at 91%.
trace: Executive sponsor engagement correlating with enterprise retention stability and renewal execution.

title: Beacon Logistics renews three months early on $280K contract
anchor: Renewal signed April 21st versus July 15th contract end date for $280K ARR.
connect: Custom Permissions and Audit Logs ships June 15th, ahead of the original renewal window.
body: Renewal signed April 21st versus July 15th contract end date for $280K ARR. Custom Permissions and Audit Logs ships June 15th, ahead of the original renewal window.
trace: Early renewal timing aligning with product launch schedule, suggesting feature roadmap influence on renewal acceleration.

title: April expansion pipeline sourcing concentrates in health reviews
anchor: Health review sourcing produces 8 expansion opportunities totaling $340K across April.
connect: March health review sourcing generated 6 opportunities with 83% sales acceptance.
body: Health review sourcing produces 8 expansion opportunities totaling $340K across April. March health review sourcing generated 6 opportunities with 83% sales acceptance.
trace: Health review process becoming primary expansion pipeline source with consistent opportunity generation.

title: Small business segment retention trails enterprise by 8 percentage points
anchor: Q2 small business GRR at 86% across $252K renewing book versus enterprise at 94%.
connect: Small business NRR sits at 102%, the lowest expansion rate across all segments.
body: Q2 small business GRR at 86% across $252K renewing book versus enterprise at 94%. Small business NRR sits at 102%, the lowest expansion rate across all segments.
trace: Small business segment showing both retention and expansion challenges relative to larger segments.

title: Tech-touch GRR improvement narrows coverage tier performance spread
anchor: Tech-touch Q2 GRR at 82% versus Q1 at 86%, while high-touch improved from 94% to 96%.
connect: Tech-touch segment represents 98 accounts across the 120-account customer base.
body: Tech-touch Q2 GRR at 82% versus Q1 at 86%, while high-touch improved from 94% to 96%. Tech-touch segment represents 98 accounts across the 120-account customer base.
trace: Tech-touch retention showing variance while representing the majority of the customer base by count.

title: Executive sponsor reviews scheduled for 2 of 2 top-ARR at-risk accounts
anchor: Top-20 ARR accounts in at-risk status total 2, both carrying executive sponsor review this week.
connect: At-risk pool concentration in top-ARR accounts dropped from 5 to 2 accounts month over month.
body: Top-20 ARR accounts in at-risk status total 2, both carrying executive sponsor review this week. At-risk pool concentration in top-ARR accounts dropped from 5 to 2 accounts month over month.
trace: High-touch executive engagement aligned with at-risk account reduction, particularly in high-value segments.

title: Multi-product expansion momentum accelerates from Q1 baseline
anchor: Multi-product Q2 NRR at 124% versus Q1 at 116%, an 8-point quarter-over-quarter increase.
connect: Single-product NRR holds steady at 102% across both quarters.
body: Multi-product Q2 NRR at 124% versus Q1 at 116%, an 8-point quarter-over-quarter increase. Single-product NRR holds steady at 102% across both quarters.
trace: Multi-product customers showing accelerating expansion while single-product remains stable, widening the adoption advantage.
